discriminating_assertions: uses-arc-runner (with_skill: PASS, without_skill: FAIL) — the skill taught the correct ARC runner label
has-timeout (with_skill: PASS, without_skill: FAIL) — the skill enforced timeout-minutes
non_discriminating_assertions: Security review assertions — both versions caught all 4 issues equally well
Debug check mismatch assertions — both versions diagnosed correctly
observations: The skill's primary value was in the benchmark workflow eval: it enforced arc-runner-set and timeout-minutes, which the baseline missed.
Security review and debugging evals showed no assertion-level difference, but the with-skill version added a missing-timeout finding and a best-practices checklist that the baseline didn't include.
The with-skill version also included a fully rewritten secure workflow, whereas the baseline only provided fixes inline.
Token cost is ~12% higher with the skill due to reading the skill file, which is a reasonable tradeoff for the quality improvement.
